programme.desc: Two fraudsters try to dupe a big gambling syndicate. However, when the syndicate comes to know their real identities, they flee to save their lives.

programme.desc: Inday endures many hardships after being abandoned by her mother. Although Inday tries to hate her mother, she feels drawn to her as they grow older.

programme.title: Tik Tak Toys: My Kolokotoys

programme.title: Last Night

programme.title: Pinulot ka Lang sa Lupa

programme.title: Jack en Poy

programme.title: Harry & Patty
programme.desc: Harry and Patty go from awkward acquaintances to head-over-heels lovers in no time. However, their fast-paced, dreamy romance hits a snag when the past starts interfering with the present.

programme.title: Maybe This Time
programme.desc: Steph learns to be tough and sets her sights on bigger dreams after Tonio ends their relationship without saying goodbye.

programme.title: Talahib At Rosas 2
programme.desc: Two childhood friends who now belong to two opposing camps cross paths once more and find themselves fighting a greedy land-grabber.

programme.title: Top Chef S17
programme.desc: In an epic Italian showdown, the final three All Stars cook the meal of their lives in an effort to take home the ultimate title and prize. To win, all they’ll need to impress not only the judges - Tom, Padma, Gail and Nilou Motamed, but also culinary superstars Mauro Colagreco, Clare Smyth, Marcus Samuelsson, Janice Wong, Tony Mantuano, Eighth Generation Italian Butcher Dario Cecchini and Food & Wine magazine’s Hunter Lewis. It’s a fight to the bitter end, but only one will have their second chance to finally be crowned Top Chef.

programme.title: Class of 3000 S1
programme.desc: When Philly Phil starts feeling like an outcast at Westley because of his poor performance in a Field Day event he transfers to a science magnet school. Missing their friend and feeling guilty for their role in making him feel unwanted the Westley kids have their work cut out for him when they try to woo him back. Meanwhile Luna is tired of being constantly upstaged by Sunny and vows to make the school think he's just as cool.

programme.title: Class of 3000 S1
programme.desc: Adventures with music superstar Sunny Bridges and his class.

programme.title: Class of 3000 S1
programme.desc: When Li'l D inadvertently offends the participants in the annual Five Points Renaissance Fair the Rennies lay siege to Westley and destroy the school's auditorium. With nowhere to stage their musical production of Rapunzel (in which Eddie will play the lead) the kids ask Eddie to convince his parents to pay for the repairs. However upon hearing about the dangerous way in which the auditorium was destroyed Eddie's parents become unreasonably overprotective of their son to the point where Eddie must decide between quitting the musical or standing up to his parents.

programme.title: Ruby and the Well S2

programme.desc: Ben and Max get into an argument and Ben goes Heatblast.

programme.desc: Gwen and Ben compete to see who can spot more birds first while birdwatching at Pinnacles National Park. A forest fire interrupts their fun and Team Tennyson must hurry to save the bird population.

programme.title: Class of 3000 S1
programme.desc: When all the Westley Kids but Philly Phil get invited to a popular kid's party Philly Phil becomes determined to figure out exactly what it is about popular kid behavior that he doesn't grasp. The data he compiles on the subject he then uses to build a robot he calls BROC (Big Robot on Campus) which he endows with all the sure-fire popularity attributes he can think of thus making it possible for Philly Phil to ride into popularity on BROC's coattails. Surprisingly Philly Phil's plan seems to work... that is until things start backfiring when BROC begins treating Philly Phil as a social liability and starts scoring popularity points at Philly Phil's expense.

programme.title: Ruby and the Well S2
programme.desc: Ruby and her friends struggle to grant the wish of a very intimidating and mysterious wisher.

programme.title: The National Dog Show ('20)
programme.desc: From the Kennel Club of Philadelphia, this cherished competition for all dog lovers returns for the 17th year. Hosted by John O'Hurley, the event features 2,000 of the nation's leading canines, including the world’s largest, smallest and most exotic. Over 170 breeds strut their stuff with the hope of becoming this year's top dog. “The Dean” David Frei provides expert show commentary, and Mary Carillo chimes in as an adept sideline reporter for this ultimate canine showdown. But which dog will have their day, and which will leave with their tail between their legs? After all, there can only be one “Best in Show”.

programme.desc: John Cena dominated wrestling for over a decade before transitioning to Hollywood, following Dwayne "The Rock" Johnson. Whether acting or wrestling, he lives by his mantra: Hustle, Loyalty, and Respect.

programme.title: Carrie Underwood: Southern Grace
programme.desc: From a small-town farm in Checotah, Oklahoma, to the biggest stages in the world — Carrie Underwood’s journey is the story of a dream, a voice, and a fire that couldn’t be ignored. This is the story of Carrie Underwood — the all-American girl who became a living legend.

programme.title: Sting: Ever Evolving
programme.desc: Sting: Ever Evolving is an enthralling documentary that delves into the multifaceted life of one of music's most iconic figures. The documentary offers an unprecedented glimpse into the mind of a musical legend, showcasing how his art continues to inspire and challenge the world.

programme.title: Kate Winslet: Leading Lady
programme.desc: Oscar-winner Kate Winslet, known for Titanic, is celebrated for her versatile, intense performances in both Hollywood blockbusters and indie films, cementing her as one of the finest actors of her generation.

programme.title: P!nk: Fearless
programme.desc: Pink is a powerhouse of pop and rock music. Although initially seen as yet another act in the late 90’s crowd of teen pop acts, she has since gone on to define her own genre and solidified her place as a standout from the rest. She is a force to be reckoned with in the music scene.

programme.title: Nicole Kidman: Outback to Oscars
programme.desc: Feature documentary about the early life, filmic career, and trials and tribulations of Australian icon, Nicole Kidman. From a Bush Christmas to Oscar winning performances, her story is one of inspiration and hardship.

programme.title: Cher: Sharing Her Story
programme.desc: Step into the world of Cher, an enduring icon whose journey from humble beginnings to superstardom has shaped pop culture for over five decades. A true pioneer, she’s dominated music charts, reinvented herself in film, and built a legacy that transcends entertainment. Cher’s influence extends beyond the stage, empowering generations with her fearlessness, resilience, and activism.

programme.title: Aerial Italy
programme.desc: They come in all shapes and sizes, are home to terrifying volcanoes, magnificent ancient civilizations, and some of the bluest waters on Earth. These are the fascinating worlds of Italy's Islands.

programme.title: Aerial Italy
programme.desc: With some of the most beautiful Greek temples on the Mediterranean, cities that just seem to float above the sea, and one of the most powerful volcanoes on Earth, this region of Italy feels like another world.

programme.title: Matthew Perry: Not Just Friends
programme.desc: Matthew Perry, beloved for his role as Chandler Bing, is remembered through archive footage highlighting his comedic legacy and personal struggles, both on and off set, as a cherished friend and human being.

programme.title: Robin Williams: Laugh until you Cry
programme.desc: The comic genius with an electric mind, with a range of talent that left us breathless. His story inspires us to look past the mask of those people who seem to have a perfect life. His legacy lives on.

programme.title: John Cena: Champion of the Ring
programme.desc: John Cena dominated wrestling for over a decade before transitioning to Hollywood, following Dwayne "The Rock" Johnson. Whether acting or wrestling, he lives by his mantra: Hustle, Loyalty, and Respect.

programme.title: Four Corners - Episode 14
programme.desc: The multi-award winning Four Corners returns for another year of powerhouse investigative journalism - fearless and forensic, exposing scandals, triggering inquiries, leading debate and holding power to account for 65 years.

programme.title: Media Watch - Episode 15
programme.desc: Australia's leading forum for media analysis and comment. Host Linton Besser, a four-time Walkley award-winning investigative reporter and former foreign correspondent, brings you a fearless critique of the week's journalism.

programme.title: Dr Karl's How Things Work - Books
programme.desc: Dr Karl pulls back the cover on how books are made, following 'The Great Gatsby' on its journey from pixels to pages at the Ligare book printers in Sydney. (Final)

programme.title: Restoration Australia - Pise Cottage, SA
programme.desc: Host Professor Anthony Burke joins a dedicated couple doing all they can to preserve a valuable slice of Australian history that would otherwise dissolve into the dirt and be lost forever.

programme.title: Larapinta - Episode 5
programme.desc: Protecting language goes hand in hand with revitalising country, and the Pertame language school is teaching a new generation. Kevin Ungwunaka introduces Talia to Running Waters, where the battle for conservation is imminent.

programme.title: Cancer: A Story of Hope - A Catalyst Special - Cancer: A Story of Hope
programme.desc: It's a diagnosis we all dread but with advances in technology and medicine, more people are living better and longer with cancer. We follow patients experiencing this new kind of care - their stories are filled with hope.

programme.title: ABC News with Joe O'Brien - Episode 100
programme.desc: Joe O'Brien brings you a comprehensive summary of the day's events, with the latest breaking news, in depth coverage of the top stories, live crosses, interviews and explainers to bring you up to speed on what's going on.

programme.title: A Bite to Eat with Alice - Joel Creasey
programme.desc: Radio presenter and comedian Joel Creasey is in the kitchen and he's brought with him frozen peas... but what deliciousness can Alice rustle up with such a humble ingredient?

programme.title: That Pacific Sports Show - Episode 15
programme.desc: Join Tiana Penitani Gray and Sam Wykes for your weekly hit of Pacific joy, as they share inspiring stories and incredible sporting prowess of athletes with Pacific Island heritage, from Australia and around the world.

programme.title: ABC National News - Episode 140
programme.desc: There's never been a more important time for transparency and trust. ABC News brings you the day's top stories and latest news, with more reporters to tell you what's going on and why it matters. Presented by Joe O'Brien.

programme.title: 7.30 - Episode 79
programme.desc: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Jacob Greber.

programme.title: Restoration Australia - Pise Cottage, SA
programme.desc: Host Professor Anthony Burke joins a dedicated couple doing all they can to preserve a valuable slice of Australian history that would otherwise dissolve into the dirt and be lost forever.
